title: cephalanthera damasonium f. spicata
intro: Cephalanthera damasonium f. spicata, also known as the lesser helleborine, is a species of orchid native to Europe and Western Asia. The lesser helleborine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin infections. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and it has been used in herbal preparations like infusions, teas, and tinctures. However, its use and preparation should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
